WTI.data_anomaly: price_source_conflict: fast_info prev_close $102.46 vs history $108.66 diverge by 5.8%. Signal suppressed.

macro_summary: Monday's session saw the S&P 500 inch up 0.18% while the Nasdaq 100 slipped 0.09% as bond yields surged — the 10-year hit 4.62% after a 30-year auction crossed 5% for the first time since 2007. Oil dropped 4.6% to $103.66 after Trump postponed a planned Iran strike, easing supply fears, but the Hormuz inventory drain remains acute. Semis fell 2.1% (SOXX 495.87) ahead of NVDA earnings, while China agreed in principle to mutual US tariff reductions, providing a modest trade-deal tailwind.
commodities_note: WTI shows conflicting price sources (fast_info vs history diverge 5.8%), so the $103.66 close is the best estimate; the 5-day trend (+1.45%) and monthly gain (+15.68%) remain intact despite the anomaly. Gold drifted 0.47% to $4,546.90 amid hawkish Fed rate-hike expectations.

support_legs: Revenue growth 123% YoY with $98M run-rate and cash cushion

singapore.summary: All three Singapore banks remain CAUTION, pinned by US10Y at 4.62% — well above the 4.20% NIM-relief threshold — and MAS S$NEER appreciation that won't be reviewed until October. DBS is the most overbought with RSI 84.9, UOB shows the least extension at 3.7% but still carries an RSI 72 block. The cluster requires a dual catalyst: lower US yields plus an MAS pivot.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally compressed — no NIM relief until US10Y <4.20% and MAS eases.
singapore.key_development: US10Y surged to 4.62% after 30-year auction crossed 5%, deepening the rate headwind and extending the NIM pain cycle.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster remains uniformly CAUTION as SOXX shed 2.1% to 495.87, extending the sell-off from record highs. NVDA earnings in two days are the pivotal event — a strong guide could stabilize the group, but every name is extended. TSM and SK Hynix offer the most compelling fundamental value (20x forward and 4.6x respectively), but their extreme extensions block entry. ASML and LITE are closest to their SMAs and best positioned for a reset.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish — AI capex demand intact — but tactically extended across the board.
semiconductors.key_development: NVDA reports in 2 days (bull case to $228); TSMC and Lumentum sold off sharply, offering the most realistic pullback paths.

big_tech.summary: BigTech is mixed: MSFT cleared the OpenAI lawsuit cloud (per Simply Wall St., May 19) but remains extended and carrying 45% OpenAI RPO concentration risk. GOOG is heavily extended after the Blackstone AI cloud venture announcement (per Reuters, May 19). PLTR was downgraded to CAUTION on valuation (66x forward PE) despite defense tailwinds. No BigTech name offers a clean entry; all are either extended or overvalued.
big_tech.thesis_status: Structurally mixed — AI revenue growth real but concentrated; valuations stretched.
big_tech.key_development: Microsoft's OpenAI trial victory removes legal overhang but does not change the credit-circularity risk.

interconnected.name: Samsung Electronics
interconnected.reason: Third HBM3E supplier after SK Hynix and Micron — Samsung's qualification status with NVDA is the key read-through on memory supply diversity.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if Samsung's HBM3E passes NVDA qualification and 000660.KS pulls back to KRW 1,195,000, reducing the duopoly risk.

interconnected.name: Coherent
interconnected.reason: Co-investee alongside LITE in NVDA's $2B photonics partnership — moves as an optics basket; today's optics selloff (per Barrons, May 18) is a sector-wide event.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if COHR pulls back 10-15% from current levels, aligning with LITE's SMA50 reset.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: Primary networking play for AI datacenter interconnects — direct beneficiary of NVDA Spectrum-X and hyperscaler capex; Cisco's strong results boosted the sector.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if NVDA earnings confirm AI networking demand and ANET pulls back 5-8% from recent highs.

active_risks: Strait of Hormuz closure persists (Week 12); US Navy blockading Iranian ports, oil inventories eroding — WTI at $103.66 after Trump postponed strike.
US-China trade: preliminary agreement to lower some tariffs on mutual basis (per China commerce ministry, May 17), but semiconductor export controls remain.
Russia hit a Chinese commercial vessel in Black Sea with a Shahed drone, escalating shipping risk.
Hawkish Fed pivot: Kevin Warsh to be sworn in Friday, rate hike bets rising after April CPI 3.8% and PPI 1.4% m/m.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil eased on the delayed Iran strike but the inventory drain is structural; energy costs continue to pressure margins. The US-China tariff deal boosted sentiment but hasn't eased chip restrictions. Tech multiples are under pressure from rising bond yields (US10Y 4.62%), keeping the semis and BigTech clusters in CAUTION.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Trump postponed planned Iran strike following requests from Gulf leaders (per CNBC, May 19); China announced preliminary tariff-reduction agreement with US (per Politico, May 17).
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Stay in cash; no new entries with 20 of 22 names hard-blocked on technical extension. Wait for NVDA earnings and the Hormuz situation to crystallize.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensive — no clean buys, wait for reset.
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: Twenty of twenty-two names are hard-blocked on technical extension — the most extreme concentration of blocked entries since the April peak, with every semis and Singapore bank shut out.
